structuredPlan.goal: Validate the 14-dimension web component scorer against 11 real libraries and helix, generating actionable scorecards and comparative reports.

acceptanceCriteria.description: All 11 wc-libraries (material, spectrum, vaadin, fluentui, carbon, ui5, calcite, porsche, ionic, wired, elix) parse and score successfully
Helix library scores successfully and generates component-by-component breakdown
All components score between 0-100 with valid grade distribution (A-F)
Cross-library comparison table ranks libraries by average score and per-dimension
CEM-Source Fidelity dimension identifies at least one real discrepancy in helix
Helix scores competitively with Material Web on CEM-native dimensions
Benchmark test completes in under 60 seconds
No test regressions in existing test suite

tasks.title: Create benchmark test infrastructure and library loader
tasks.description: Set up tests/benchmark/cross-library-scorer.test.ts with functions to load custom-elements.json from all 11 wc-libraries and helix library. Implement robust error handling for malformed or missing files.

tasks.title: Implement component scoring across all libraries
tasks.description: For each library, score ALL components using scoreComponentMultiDimensional() across all 14 dimensions. Collect results with timestamps and scorer version for tracking.

tasks.title: Generate per-library scorecards with dimension insights
tasks.description: For each library, produce a scorecard containing: average score, grade distribution, dimension averages, top 5/bottom 5 components, dimension-level insights identifying which dimensions drag scores down, and confidence breakdown (verified/heuristic/untested).

tasks.title: Build cross-library comparison table
tasks.description: Generate a comparison table ranking all 12 libraries (11 wc + helix) by average score, with per-dimension rankings. Identify which dimensions differentiate high vs low scoring libraries.

tasks.title: Create helix-specific deep-dive report
tasks.description: Generate tests/benchmark/helix-report.test.ts with full component-by-component breakdown, issues found per component (especially from CEM-Source Fidelity), comparison against Material Web and Carbon as gold standards, and actionable issue list for helix team.

tasks.title: Implement comprehensive test assertions
tasks.description: Add assertions verifying: all libraries parse without errors, all components score 0-100, grade distribution is sensible, CEM-Source Fidelity catches real discrepancies, helix is competitive with Material Web on CEM-native dimensions.

tasks.title: Set up performance gate and output formatting
tasks.description: Configure benchmark to complete in under 60 seconds with performance monitoring. Format output for both stdout (vitest) and optional JSON fixtures at tests/__fixtures__/benchmark-results/ for historical comparison.

tasks.title: Document benchmark methodology and results interpretation
tasks.description: Add README.md in tests/benchmark/ explaining how to run benchmarks, interpret results, understand dimension scores, and use helix report for actionable fixes.
tasks.verifyCommand: test -f tests/benchmark/README.md && wc -l tests/benchmark/README.md | awk '{print $1}' | grep -E '^[0-9]+$'

deviationRules.condition: A library's custom-elements.json is missing or malformed
deviationRules.action: Log a clear error message, skip that library, but continue with remaining libraries. Report skipped libraries in test summary.

deviationRules.condition: CEM-Source Fidelity analyzer (F1, F2, or F3) has not been merged yet
deviationRules.action: Skip that dimension in scoring, mark it as 'awaiting merger' in reports, and continue with other 13 dimensions.
Implement CEM parsing cache with in-memory storage, skip full re-parsing on subsequent runs, and add benchmarking logs to identify bottleneck.

deviationRules.condition: Helix library path or custom-elements.json structure differs from expected
deviationRules.action: Add fallback path detection and log exact path being loaded. Fail the helix-specific test with clear error rather than silently missing helix validation.

deviationRules.condition: A component's score is outside 0-100 range
deviationRules.action: Throw an assertion error with component name, library, dimension values, and scorer version. Do not silently clamp or round values.
